halfLife.conditions: extensive (normal) CYP2D6 metabolisers; mean elimination half-life 3–4 h. Poor CYP2D6 metabolisers run 7–9 h — a discrete genotype subpopulation recorded under variability, deliberately NOT merged into this continuous band (it is bimodal, not a smooth spread).

vd.conditions: extensively distributed; reported volume 3.2–5.6 L/kg. Taken as a true volume (reported independently of oral F) and scaled to absolute litres against the 70 kg reference subject at derivation time; point value is the mid-range.

F.conditions: ~50% oral bioavailability due to saturable pre-systemic (first-pass) hepatic metabolism; because first-pass is saturable, exposure rises NON-proportionately with dose (see notes) — F is treated as fixed at 50% for the illustrative therapeutic dose

tmax.conditions: peak plasma concentration reached 1–2 h after oral immediate-release dosing; representative point value 1.5 h

variability.notes: Metoprolol is extensively metabolised by CYP2D6, which is genetically polymorphic. Poor metabolisers (absent CYP2D6 activity — about 8% of Caucasians, ~2% of most other populations) show several-fold higher plasma concentrations and a longer half-life (7–9 h vs 3–4 h in extensive metabolisers). This bimodal genotype effect is the dominant source of inter-individual variability and is the reason metoprolol is a flagged future genotype-example compound (§12) alongside diphenhydramine; it is intentionally kept separate from the continuous half-life band, which reflects only the extensive-metaboliser range.

fda_label.title: Metoprolol Tartrate Tablet, Film Coated (Mylan Pharmaceuticals) — FDA Structured Product Label, clinical pharmacology

statpearls.title: Morris J, Awosika AO, Dunham A. Metoprolol. StatPearls [Internet]. Treasure Island (FL): StatPearls Publishing; updated 2024 Feb 29 (oral Tmax)

notes: Curated primarily from the FDA Structured Product Label for immediate-release metoprolol tartrate (Mylan), with the oral Tmax taken from the StatPearls Metoprolol monograph (the label states beta-blockade within 1 h but gives no numeric Tmax). Reported values: elimination half-life 3–4 h in extensive CYP2D6 metabolisers, volume of distribution 3.2–5.6 L/kg, oral bioavailability ~50%, Tmax 1–2 h, low (~10–12%) protein binding. ke is taken from the extensive-metaboliser half-life (~3.5 h). The KEY nonlinearity note: the label states pre-systemic (first-pass) metabolism is SATURABLE, so oral exposure increases non-proportionately as the DOSE is raised (higher fraction escapes first-pass at higher doses). Like metformin, however, this nonlinearity lives in absorption/first-pass, NOT in elimination — elimination remains first-order with a dose-independent half-life — so superposition of IDENTICAL repeated doses (each with the same F and disposition) stays valid, and linear: true holds for fixed-dose multiple dosing. The entry must not be used to extrapolate across different dose sizes. Vd is kept as a true volume with explicit oral F = 0.5 (not folded in), so the oral curve is C = F·D/V; IV routes (metoprolol tartrate injection is a real product) use F = 1 by definition. CYP2D6 poor-metaboliser status is the dominant real-world variability driver and is recorded under variability.geneticFactors rather than smeared into the half-life band. Educational illustration only — not for dosing.
